heading: Support of special operations to combat terrorism
label: 127e Support of special operations to combat terrorism

E, title LVII, § 5703135 Stat. 2382Dec. 27, 2021“In the course of a chief of mission providing concurrence to the exercise of the authority pursuant to , United States Code, or section 1202 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018 [; ]—section 127e of title 10Pub. L. 115–91131 Stat. 1639“(1)each relevant chief of mission shall inform and consult in a timely manner with relevant individuals at relevant missions or bureaus of the Department of State; and“(2)the Secretary of State shall take such steps as may be necessary to ensure that such relevant individuals have the security clearances necessary and access to relevant compartmented and special programs to so consult in a timely manner with respect to such concurrence.”

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may, with the concurrence of the relevant Chief of Mission, expend up to $100,000,000 during any fiscal year to provide support to foreign forces, irregular forces, groups, or individuals engaged in supporting or facilitating authorized ongoing military operations by United States special operations forces to combat terrorism.

children.heading: .—Funds
children.label: b .—Funds
children.text: Funds for support under this section in a fiscal year shall be derived from amounts authorized to be appropriated for that fiscal year for the Department of Defense for operation and maintenance.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The authority in this section shall be exercised in accordance with such procedures as the Secretary shall establish for purposes of this section. The Secretary shall notify the congressional defense committees of any material change to such procedures.

children.label: A
children.text: Policy, strategy, or other guidance for the execution of, and constraints within, activities conducted under this section.

children.label: B
children.text: The processes through which activities conducted under this section are to be developed, validated, and coordinated, as appropriate, with relevant Federal entities.

children.label: C
children.text: The processes through which legal reviews and determinations are made to comply with this section and ensure that the exercise of the authority in this section is consistent with the national security of the United States.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 15 days before exercising the authority in this section to make funds available to initiate support of an approved military operation or changing the scope or funding level of any support for such an operation by $1,000,000 or an amount equal to 20 percent of such funding level (whichever is less), or not later than 48 hours after exercising such authority if the Secretary determines that extraordinary circumstances that impact the national security of the United States exist, the Secretary shall notify the congressional defense committees of the use of such authority with respect to that operation. Any such notification shall be in writing.

children.label: A
children.text: The type of support provided or to be provided to United States special operations forces and a description of the authorized ongoing operation.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the foreign forces, irregular forces, groups, or individuals engaged in supporting or facilitating the authorized ongoing operation who will receive support provided under this section.

children.label: C
children.text: A detailed description of the support provided or to be provided to the recipient.

children.label: D
children.text: The amount obligated under the authority to provide support.

children.label: E
children.text: A detailed description of the legal and operational authorities related to the authorized ongoing operation, including relevant execute orders issued by the Secretary of Defense and combatant commanders related to the authorized ongoing operation, including an identification of operational activities United States Special Operations Forces are authorized to conduct under such execute orders.

children.label: F
children.text: The duration for which the support is expected to be provided and an identification of the timeframe in which the provision of support will be reviewed by the combatant commander for a determination regarding the necessity of continuation of support.

children.label: G
children.text: A description of the entities with which the recipients of support are engaged in hostilities and whether each such entity is covered under an authorization for use of military force.

children.label: H
children.text: A description of the steps taken to ensure the support is consistent with United States national security objectives.

children.label: I
children.text: A description of the steps taken to ensure that the recipients of support have not engaged in human rights violations.

children.heading: Notification of Suspension or Termination of Support.—
children.label: e Notification of Suspension or Termination of Support.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 48 hours after suspending or terminating support to any foreign force, irregular force, group, or individual under the authority in this section, the Secretary shall submit to the congressional defense committees a written notice of such suspension or termination.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the reasons for the suspension or termination of such support.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of any effects on regional, theatre, or global campaign plan objectives anticipated to result from the suspension or termination of such support.

children.label: C
children.text: A plan for the suspension or termination of the support, and, in the case of support that is planned to be transitioned to another program of the Department of Defense or another Federal department or agency, a detailed description of the transition plan, including the resources, equipment, capabilities, and personnel associated with such plan.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Delegation
children.label: f .—Limitation on Delegation
children.text: The authority of the Secretary to make funds available under this section for support of a military operation may not be delegated.
A covert action, as such term is defined in section 503(e) of the National Security Act of 1947 ().50 U.S.C. 3093(e)

An introduction of the armed forces, (including as such term is defined in section 8(c) of the War Powers Resolution (), into hostilities, or into situations where hostilities are clearly indicated by the circumstances, without specific statutory authorization within the meaning of section 5(b) of such Resolution ().50 U.S.C. 1547(c)50 U.S.C. 1544(b)
Activities or support of activities, directly or indirectly, that are inconsistent with the laws of armed conflict.

children.heading: .—Oversight by ASD for SOLIC
children.label: h .—Oversight by ASD for SOLIC
children.text: The Assistant Secretary of Defense for Special Operations and Low Intensity Conflict shall have primary responsibility within the Office of the Secretary of Defense for oversight of policies and programs for support authorized by this section.

children.heading: .—Report on preceding calendar year
children.label: 1 .—Report on preceding calendar year
children.text: Not later than 120 days after the last day of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall submit to the congressional defense committees a report on the support provided under this section during the preceding calendar year.

children.heading: .—Report on current calendar year
children.label: 2 .—Report on current calendar year
children.text: Not later than six months after the date of the submittal of the report most recently submitted under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall submit to the congressional defense committees a report on the support provided under this section during the first half of the calendar year in which the report under this paragraph is submitted.

children.label: A
children.text: A summary of the ongoing military operations by United States special operations forces to combat terrorism that were supported or facilitated by foreign forces, irregular forces, groups, or individuals for which support was provided under this section.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the support or facilitation provided by such foreign forces, irregular forces, groups, or individuals to United States special operations forces.

children.label: C
children.text: The type of recipients that were provided support under this section, identified by authorized category (foreign forces, irregular forces, groups, or individuals).

children.label: D
children.text: The total amount obligated for support under this section, including budget details.

children.label: E
children.text: The total amount obligated in prior fiscal years under this section and applicable preceding authority.

children.label: F
children.text: The intended duration of support provided under this section.

children.label: G
children.text: A description of the support or training provided to the recipients of support under this section.

children.label: H
children.text: A value assessment of the support provided under this section, including a summary of significant activities undertaken by foreign forces, irregular forces, groups, or individuals to support operations by United States special operations forces to combat terrorism.
